commit.message:
feat(api): send team concurrency with FirePDF async submits (#4051)

* feat(api): send team concurrency with FirePDF async submits

FirePDF's per-team admission (its ENG-5049) runs in observation mode
and needs the account context to measure anything. Submit now carries
team_concurrency from the cached ACUC lookup, best-effort: lookup
failure or absence never blocks a scrape — FirePDF simply skips team
observation for that submit. team_tier is deliberately NOT sent: no
plan/tier string exists in this package today; FirePDF defaults the
tier for observation, and the authoritative tier source is being
decided alongside its entitlement policy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: snapshot team concurrency via internalOptions, not a re-fetch

Per review: the ACUC is already in hand at the controller, so thread
it down instead of re-reading in the worker. teamConcurrency is
snapshotted from req.acuc at the same two sites that snapshot
teamFlags (v2 scrape + parse) and rides the job payload; the FirePDF
async engine reads meta.internalOptions.teamConcurrency and does no
lookup. Also pins the entitlement to acceptance time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(api): confirm Exchange billing from the billing worker (#4043)

* refactor(api): confirm Exchange billing from the billing worker

The billing confirmation moves from an awaited call in the scrape
worker to the billing pipeline, so confirmed means the debit actually
committed:

- billScrapeJob attaches the access event id to the bill_team job and
  no longer awaits anything Exchange-related
- the billing worker threads it into the batch operation with the
  billing job id as the reference
- the batch processor confirms each covered access once the group's
  debit commits, and voids it when the commit fails or refunds
- outcome reporting rides the billing pipeline's retries and never
  throws, so it cannot affect billing itself

The scrape worker's failure-path void can now race the async confirm;
the Exchange resolves this authoritatively (void -> confirmed is a
legal repair, confirmed -> void stays rejected), so a paid access can
never end up voided.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(exchange): never void on ambiguous commits, retry outcome reports

- Failed or thrown billing commits no longer void the Exchange access
  event: supaBillTeam maps transport errors to success: false, and the
  debit may have committed before the error, so voiding could erase a
  real debit. The event stays pending for reconciliation.
- Confirmation delivery moved outside the batch lock so an Exchange
  outage can never hold the 30s lease past expiry and overlap another
  batch run.
- reportExchangeBilling now retries transient failures (3 attempts,
  backoff), treats 4xx as definitive, and returns success.
- Billing-queue enqueue failure now voids the access event (the debit
  can never commit; a lost-ack enqueue is repaired by void -> confirmed).
- Index-worker guards against an empty-string access event ID.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(exchange): jittered backoff, bounded confirm concurrency, retryable 429

- Retry backoff gains full jitter (capped at 15s) so a batch of reports
  failing together does not retry in synchronized bursts against a
  degraded Exchange.
- Batch confirmations run at most 16 at a time instead of one
  unbounded Promise.all over the whole batch.
- 429 is treated as transient (honoring Retry-After when sane) instead
  of a definitive rejection, so rate-limited confirmations reconcile
  once the limit expires.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(exchange): worker-level confirm semaphore, HTTP-date Retry-After, true full jitter

- The 16-slot confirmation concurrency budget is now shared across the
  worker via a small semaphore, so overlapping post-lock deliveries
  cannot stack beyond the bound.
- Retry-After parsing accepts the HTTP-date form in addition to
  delta-seconds.
- The retry delay is now genuine full jitter (0..backoff) with
  Retry-After as the lower bound, matching the comment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(exchange): pull-based confirm worker pool

Confirmation delivery now uses a small worker pool that pulls
operations one at a time, so a slow Exchange queues at most 16 waiters
per batch on the shared budget instead of one closure per operation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(api): route eligible scrapes through the Exchange (#4030)

* feat(api): route eligible scrapes through the Exchange

Reimplement the removed data-layer path against the Exchange service:

- the provider catalog (GET /v1/providers) supplies URL routes,
  per-access credit cost, and required legal terms, cached with a
  short TTL; new providers need no changes in this repo
- access gating is generic: the org's data-source access entry is
  looked up by provider id and compared against the terms the catalog
  declares
- billing uses the creditsCost reported per response, and each billed
  access is confirmed back to the Exchange so its ledger reconciles
- terms-required responses carry the provider's terms key and version
  from the catalog

Configured via EXCHANGE_URL; the beta flag and the org data-source
access flags are unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(api): serve the stale Exchange catalog while refreshing

Catalog expiry no longer adds latency to flagged-org requests: the
expired catalog is served immediately while the refresh runs in the
background. Only the very first lookup after boot waits for the fetch.
Orgs without the beta flag never reach the catalog at all - the flag
check is the first, synchronous eligibility gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): degrade gracefully when the Exchange is unavailable

Two hardening changes so an Exchange outage or a bug in its gate can
never affect the normal scrape path:

- a failed catalog refresh keeps serving the last good catalog instead
  of clobbering it with null; the failure TTL only throttles the next
  refresh attempt
- the access gate wraps everything in a fail-safe: any unexpected error
  degrades the request to ineligible (normal blocklist behavior) with a
  warning log, instead of failing the customer's request

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): address review findings on the Exchange path

- void the Exchange access event when a delivered scrape ultimately
  fails (post-delivery crawl rejection), so the ledger reconciles a
  never-billed access instead of leaving it pending
- fail closed in buildFallbackList when a blocklisted URL reaches the
  worker but the Exchange is no longer usable (bypass TOCTOU); scoped
  to flagged orgs and fail-safe so it can never break ordinary scrapes
- respect internalOptions.forceEngine over Exchange routing
- drop deterministicJson from supported formats - its extractors need
  page HTML, which Exchange responses do not carry
- remove .catch(0) on credit costs in both schemas: malformed costs now
  reject loudly instead of silently billing 0
- synthesize a minimal HTML head from the provider's title/description
  so document metadata is populated
- validate EXCHANGE_URL as a URL at config load
- strip embedded credentials from logged URLs
- drop the redundant lockdown term from the middleware ZDR computation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(api): rename the Exchange env var to FIRE_EXCHANGE_URL

Matches the service's deployment naming (fire-exchange).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): fail closed when the Exchange blocklist re-check errors

For flagged orgs, an error in the worker-side blocklist re-check now
returns no engines instead of continuing: a retryable scrape failure is
preferable to normal engines scraping a potentially blocklisted site
that was admitted through the Exchange bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): address second review round on the Exchange path

- crawl bodies nest options under scrapeOptions; eligibility now reads
  the effective per-page options so ineligible crawls cannot bypass
  the blocklist
- profile-backed scrapes are Exchange-ineligible (session-specific
  content cannot come from a provider)
- the engine span uses the credential-redacted URL, matching the log
  context
- the billing confirmation is awaited before billScrapeJob returns, so
  a later failure-path void can never race it; the service rejects
  confirmed->void, making the order decisive

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(api): warm the Exchange catalog at process startup

The API server and scrape worker prefetch the provider catalog at
boot, so the first flagged-org request never waits on the catalog
fetch; stale-while-revalidate keeps every later lookup in-memory.
No-op when FIRE_EXCHANGE_URL is not configured.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): keep atsv requests off the Exchange path

The exchange engine declares atsv unsupported, but the eligibility gate
never checked it, so atsv-enabled scrapes of provider URLs were routed
exclusively to the Exchange and the flag was silently ignored. atsv now
makes a request Exchange-ineligible, checked in the middleware and in
engine selection like every other engine-shaping option.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): never store Exchange content on the Firecrawl side

Exchange-delivered documents are excluded from the index cache and the
search index: every access to provider content must go through the
Exchange and its ledger, and no copy may persist outside it.

Also from review:
- minAge requests are Exchange-ineligible - they ask for
  Firecrawl-cached data, which never exists for Exchange content
- includeTags/excludeTags make a request Exchange-ineligible; selector
  filtering does not apply to provider records
- provider route prefixes match on path segment boundaries, so
  /person no longer matches /personality

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): scope the Exchange blocklist bypass to scrape-shaped routes

The Exchange gate now runs only for scrape, crawl, and batch scrape -
the flows whose jobs the exchange engine can actually serve. Map,
search, monitors, and other routes keep plain blocklist behavior, so a
provider URL can no longer bypass the blocklist on flows that never
route through the Exchange, and terms errors no longer surface there.

Also removes the unused isExchangeSupportedUrl wrapper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(api): honor Exchange access in batch scrape URL validation

Batch bodies carry urls[], so the middleware gate never applied and the
controllers' per-URL blocklist checks rejected Exchange-eligible URLs
as unsupported (or dropped them into invalidURLs). Both v1 and v2 batch
controllers now consult the Exchange gate for blocked URLs only:
allowed URLs are admitted, missing terms surface the structured terms
response, and everything else keeps existing blocklist behavior. The
gate-input mapping is shared with the middleware via
lib/exchange-request.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* revert(api): keep batch scrape off the Exchange path

Batch is out of scope for the pilot: queued batch jobs would need the
access flags threaded through internalOptions for the worker-side
recheck to hold, so rather than half-supporting it, batch keeps plain
blocklist behavior with no Exchange semantics. Only single scrape and
crawl are Exchange-aware. The shared gate-input helper stays, used by
the middleware.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(extract): delete the dead legacy extraction-service (#4019)

The main extraction-service `performExtraction` is dead code: it is declared
`async function` (not exported) and has no callers anywhere in the repo — the
live extract path is entirely `performExtraction_F0` (fire-0). Its only live
export was the `ExtractResult` type, which moves verbatim to
`lib/extract/types.ts` with its three importers (v1/extract, webhook/types,
extract-worker) repointed.

Deleting `performExtraction` orphaned its private helper tree — the pre-fire-0
extraction pipeline, of which fire-0 has its own copies. Removed per knip:
url-processor, reranker, completions/{analyzeSchemaAndPrompt,batchExtract,
singleAnswer}, usage/llm-cost, four now-unused build-prompts exports, and the
unused `MULTI_ENTITY_AGENT_SCRAPE` ExtractStep member.

Pure dead-code removal (~2,300 lines); no behavior change. tsc + knip clean;
billing suites green.

chore(billing): remove the idle credit-usage tally loop (#4010)

The index-worker ran tallyBilling() every 5min: it drained the
`billed_teams` Redis set and called update_tally_10_team for each team to
aggregate credit_usage -> credit_usage_tally. #3992 removed the two sadd
feeders of `billed_teams` (Autumn is the source of truth for credits, and
the tally's readers have been migrated off it), so the set is permanently
empty and the loop is dead work.

clearInterval), and the now-unused updateTallyTeam wrapper in db/rpc.ts.

commit.message:
ci(php-sdk): extract SDK_VERSION with the same permissive pattern as the version gate

The tag step's sed required exactly three numeric components, but
check_version_has_incremented.py extracts any quoted string — so a
pre-release like 1.10.0-beta.1 would pass the gate and then fail the job
before tagging and the Packagist notify. Capture whatever the gate
approved instead; the gate's version parse remains the format validator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(php-sdk): note why assertSuccess covers only synchronous endpoints

Review flagged the asymmetry: crawl start/status skip the success:false
envelope check. That is deliberate — a failed start yields a null job ID
that pollCrawl() and the crawl tool both guard, and crawl status is
surfaced explicitly on CrawlJob — but nothing said so.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(billing): wire batch billing onto bill_team_7 (#4008)

bill_team_7 (firecrawl-db #214) drops bill_team_6's fetch_subscription
param and its subscription self-fetch. That self-fetch was already dead
in the live path: billTeam -> queueBillingOperation stores
`subscription_id ?? null`, so by the time supaBillTeam runs, the value is
string|null (never undefined) and `fetch_subscription: subscription_id
=== undefined` was always false. So this swap is behavior-preserving --
it just deletes the unused lookup capability.

Renames the db/rpc wrapper billTeam6 -> billTeam7 (one fewer param) and
points supaBillTeam at it. bill_team_6 stays in the DB for backward compat.

Does NOT yet retire ACUC sub_id: ~20 controllers still thread
req.acuc?.sub_id into billTeam, stamping it onto credit_usage.subscription_id.
Removing those + dropping sub_id from the ACUC is the follow-up.

Co-authored-by: micahstairs <micah@sideguide.dev>

commit.message:
ci(php-sdk): tag releases on firecrawl-php so Packagist publishes stable versions

The publish workflow force-pushed the subtree split to firecrawl-php main
and notified Packagist, but never created a version tag. Packagist derives
stable versions from tags, so the package exposed only dev-main and
constraints like ^1.9 could not resolve. Push the split as
refs/tags/v{SDK_VERSION} before the Packagist notify, skipping when the tag
already exists so workflow re-runs stay idempotent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore(billing): stop populating the billed_teams tally set (#3992)

billTeam/batch_billing and the ACUC cache-clear path added each team to the
Redis "billed_teams" set, which the index-worker loop drained into
update_tally_10_team (the legacy credit_usage_tally pipeline). Autumn is the
source of truth for credits now; that tally, coupons decrement, and
credit_usage.tallied are no longer authoritative. Stop feeding the set so the
loop idles. The set can only shrink from here (drained by the still-running
loop), so nothing piles up.

Removes the now-unused getRedisConnection import from auth.ts.

fix(php-sdk): bound crawl wait time for queue-friendly execution

FirecrawlClient::crawl() polls for up to 300 seconds by default, but
Laravel queue workers commonly kill jobs at 60 seconds, orphaning a
crawl that keeps running (and billing) on the server. Add an
overridable $timeoutSeconds (default 55) to FirecrawlCrawl, catch
JobTimeoutException in handle() to return a specific, actionable
message instead of a generic guard() error, and document the wait in
the tool description and README.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(php-sdk): cap Laravel AI tool output sizes

A large scraped page or crawl result could overflow the model context
or a consumer's conversation storage. Add a shared truncate() helper
on FirecrawlTool and apply it: scrape output caps at 80000 characters,
each crawled page's markdown caps at 15000, and the map tool's limit
(both the runtime clamp and the schema max) drops from 5000 to 500.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(php-sdk): use underscore-prefixed integration tag for old API compatibility

Prefix the Laravel AI tools' integration tag with an underscore
(_laravel-ai) instead of adding a new value to the API's integration
enum. The API's integration validator accepts any underscore-prefixed
value on every deployed version, so the tools work against lagging or
self-hosted APIs without a deploy ordering requirement. Revert the
apps/api enum addition to match origin/main.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(php-sdk): surface success=false API envelopes instead of empty results

The API can return HTTP 200 with a success:false envelope (e.g. DNS
failures), which the client was hydrating into an empty Document,
SearchData, or MapData instead of raising the error. Add an
assertSuccess helper and apply it to scrape, search, and map, the
synchronous endpoints the Laravel tools call directly, so failures
surface to the model instead of reading as "no content."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(posthog): detect CLI surface via integration, not just origin

originToSurface() classified requests by `origin` alone, but the firecrawl
CLI sets `integration: "cli"` and leaves `origin` at the "api" default on its
high-volume commands (scrape/crawl/search/map/agent/parse/browser) — only
feedback/interact/search-feedback also set `origin: "cli"`. So the bulk of CLI
traffic was miscounted as `api` in the `api_surface_first_used` milestone.

- originToSurface now takes (origin, integration) and checks `integration === "cli"` first
- thread request.integration through trackFirstSurfaceUse + log_job caller
- emit raw_integration on the event for debuggability
- drop the speculative `firecrawl-cli` origin match (no client ever sends it)

Verified against firecrawl/cli command sources.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(js-sdk): send origin=js-sdk@<version> on research endpoints

Research endpoints are GETs, so the POST-body origin injection in
HttpClient never applied and requests were logged with origin="api".
Attach origin as a query param in the research methods instead
(accepted by the v2 research proxy's commonQuery schema). Bump to 4.29.2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
